title: Homeless Drop In Centers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Homeless Drop In Centers 49 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Families Pregnant Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to music lessons, art workshops, recording studio sessions, roleplaying game sessions, cultural outings, meals, hygiene supplies, clothing, first aid supplies, and mentorship services. 16 NW Broadway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Homeless youth ages 15-25 Hours: Monday-Thursday 4pm-8pm, Friday by appointment only Email (503) 481-4970 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides drop-in services including information and referral, showers, laundry facilities, a community meal, snacks, and coffee. Offers access to a phone, community voicemail, mailing address, and computers. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Non-parenting women age 25 and older who are experiencing homelessness. Well-mannered pets are allowed. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Lunch: 11:30am-1pm Email (971) 222-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A collaborative project that provides homeless families priority access to a coordinated set of services offered at multiple locations in the G Street neighborhood. The participating organizations function as a campus, with all services within easy walking distance. Services include: -A safe place to be during the day -Access to laundry, showers, and restroom facilities -A place to prepare and eat simple meals -Help finding affordable housing and employment -Access to additional school and community based resources 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness and at least one child age 17 and younger in their care Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, hygiene supplies, laundry and shower access, and a place to get relief from the weather or otherwise rest. Also may provide referrals and information about housing, healthcare, employment, and other community resources in addition to case management services. 504 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 538-9761 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SUPPORTED SHELTERS ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center offering limited respite from the elements, outreach programming, hygiene supplies, and other social supports. 1845 W 11th Street Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 683-0836 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORE COMMUNITY ROOM ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to hot meals, device charging, propane tank exchange, access to laptops for completing applications for jobs, housing and other social services, and connection and referrals to local social service providers. 222 Trinity Way Sisters, OR 97759 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 9am-1pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center providing a space to socialize, have coffee and snacks, receive mail, phone charging, employment support, information from local health and social organizations, and counseling. Provides assistance with acquiring clothing and shoes; personal hygiene items; medical and dental fees/services; and can assist with transportation needs. Also offers navigation services such as applying for SNAP; completing, obtaining, or replacing official documents like driver's license and birth certificates; access to health providers; housing assistance wait lists; and obtaining a GED. 530 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income individuals and families; individuals with disabilities Hours: Basic needs: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Social Services support: Monday-Friday noon-2pm by appointment Email (458) 233-5327 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DO GOOD MULTNOMAH NORTH PORTLAND DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a homeless drop-in center providing showers, temporary mail services, prepackaged food that can be eaten at the center or taken to go, and case management services. 9000 N Lombard Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing or at risk of homelessness. Pets allowed. Hours: Drop-in Center: Monday-Friday 9am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Showers: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (503) 545-3499 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 12685 SW 4th Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-5pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Thursday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK MADRAS SHELTER SERVICE CENTER MADRAS SHELTER DAYTIME SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center, meals, case management, showers, and laundry services. Volunteer opportunities available. 61 NW Oak Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted with a focus on individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Drop in center: 7 days per week 7am-1pm and 6pm-10pm Lunch: 7 days per week 11am-12:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 6pm-8pm Showers: Monday/Friday 7:30am-12:30pm Laundry: Thursdays 7am-1pm drop off, Friday pick up times vary Email (458) 800-4208 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center. Services include access to showers, bathrooms, mail pick up and PO Box setup, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, on-site WiFi and telephone usage, and specialized information and referral. 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted with priority given to homeless individuals or families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (503) 232-2031 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESS DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A day center that provides meals, a place to rest and socialize, housing resource materials, and WiFi. On-site housing liaison is available during the week. Mental health services are sometimes available on-site through partner agency. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Day center hours: 7 days per week 7am-7pm -Breakfast 9am-10:15am -Lunch noon-1pm -Social Services 1pm-2pm -Showers by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with access to food, clothing, and other vital resources. Provides case management services to assist youth in finding and maintaining stable housing. Also helps with employment including resume preparation, job skills training, and connections to employment opportunities. 620 E Whiteaker Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Youth ages 11-21 who are homeless, runaways, or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 767-3823 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY ARCHES PROJECT ARCHES DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in day center with services including showers, laundry, hygiene kits, meals, mailboxes, and resource navigation. 615 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Day center and meals: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-4pm, breakfast 9am-10am, lunch noon-1pm Mail pick up: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Email (503) 399-9080 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier day center that offers computers and charging stations, a temporary mailing address, laundry facilities, restrooms, showers, and resources. Access to the day center is in four, 3-hour time blocks. To enter, participants need a ticket, and 25 tickets are distributed for each time block on a first-come, first-served basis. No ticket is required to pick up mail, which is available up to four times per day. Do Good Multnomah runs shelter and bridge housing programs on site to help people continue on their path to permanent housing. Referrals come through the Day Center. 333 SW Park Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, pets allowed Hours: Day Center: 7 days per week 8am-8pm (intake stops at 7:45pm), closed the 4th Friday of the month from noon-3pm Mail pick up: 9am-10am, noon-1pm, 3pm-4pm, 6pm-7pm Time Block 1: 8am-10:45am Time Block 2: 11am-1:45pm Time Block 3: 2pm-4:45pm Time Block 4: 5pm-7:45pm Email (503) 988-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEWMAN UNITED METHODIST CHURCH COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. May provide resources such as socks, clothing, and snacks. If unable to provide the requested resource, may offer information and referrals to other agencies. 132 NE B Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Thursdays 2pm-5pm Email (541) 479-5369 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Services include information and referral, laundry, clothing, showers, lunch, mailbox access, mental health support groups, and peer support services. Also connects clients with medical services at West Salem Medical Clinic. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPEN DOOR HOUSINGWORKS UNHOUSED DAYTIME ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with access to hot meals, food boxes, showers, personal hygiene items, telephones, mail services, and case management support. 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Highway Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Food Boxes: Unrestricted Homeless services: Income must be at or below 50% Area Medan Income (AMI) Other services: Individuals experiencing or at-risk of homelessness Hours: Day center services: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm Hot meals: Monday-Friday noon-1pm Food boxes: Monday-Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 640-6689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION NIGHTWATCH DOWNTOWN HOSPITALITY CENTER HOSPITALITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a safe place where guests can drop in and socialize, play games, and read books. The center provides water, coffee, a hot meal, blankets, socks, and mental health services. 1432 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: People experiencing housing insecurity Hours: Thursday-Saturday 6:30pm-10pm Email (503) 220-0438 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center for sheltering, hot meals, haircuts, clothing, public restrooms, bus passes, wound care, hygiene kits, OHP and SNAP form assistance, ID and birth record replacement and payment assistance, resources for housing navigation and shelter referrals, detox referrals, overdose prevention, and Narcan supplies. Community Health Workers (CHWs), Peer Wellness Specialists (PWS), and Peer Support Specialists (PSS) are available to assist with resources, connections to health care, and case management. 247 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Day Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm, Thanksgiving and Christmas 9am-noon Hot meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Haircuts: Monday 10am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION BURNSIDE SHELTER GUEST CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides drop-in services including hot meals, mail services, restrooms, sinks, hygiene products, socks, water fountain, water refill station, crisis care, and information about houseless services. 111 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing houselessness for most services. Meals are open to families experiencing houselessness as well. Hours: Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Dinner: 7 days per week 6pm All other services: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 647-7466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, showers, laundry, temporary mailing addresses, clothing provisions, cell phone charging, TriMet tickets, shelter and housing referrals, and connections to various community partners and services. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who are homeless Hours: Center: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Meals: Breakfast 9am, sack lunches noon, hot meal 3pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO SHORT-TERM SLEEPING AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a tented area for individuals to sleep. Basic sleeping materials are available for those who do not have them. Guests may stay for 12 hours and may return after a 12-hour break. Also offers a clothing closet. Item availability depends on what has been donated and is on hand at any given time. 999 N Thunderbird Way Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with exceptions for legally emancipated teens Hours: Line up and sign up at 5pm. Check in at 9pm. 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (closed Sunday 7am-8pm) Clothing closet: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 10am-4pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHEPHERD'S HOUSE MINISTRIES LOW-BARRIER SHELTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter, community meals, showers, laundry, and a clothing closet. Case management services in regard to housing, employment, recovery, mental health care, medical treatment, and life goals may be available on a case-by-case basis. 145 NE Revere Avenue Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Adults aged 18 and older and families with minorchildren. Unaccompanied youth under 18 not eligible. Well-behaved pets allowed with owner supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Meals 7 days per week: Breakfast 7am-8am; Lunch Noon-1pm; Dinner 6pm-7pm Shelter check-in 7 days per week: 5:30pm-10pm; assigned beds guaranteed only until 8pm Discussion/instruction hours: Monday-Friday 8am-11am Email (541) 388-2096 - 830-5 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHEPHERD'S HOUSE MINISTRIES SHEPHERD'S HOUSE MINISTRIES REDMOND LOW-BARRIER SHELTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides overnight shelter, community meals 3 times a day, showers, laundry, commercial kitchen, and an emergency clothing closet. Individual case management services for housing, employment, recovery, mental health care, medical treatment, and life goals are also available on a case-by-case basis. Residents can participate in faith based fellowship and discussion groups. Donations for clothing and household type items accepted. 1350 S Highway 97 Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Adults aged 18 and older and families with minorchildren. Unaccompanied youth under 18 not eligible. Well-behaved pets allowed with owner supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Meals 7 days per week: Breakfast 7am-8am; Lunch Noon-1pm; Dinner 6pm-7pm Shelter check-in 7 days per week: 5:30pm-10pm; assigned beds guaranteed only until 8pm Discussion/instruction hours: Monday-Friday 8am-11am Email (541) 548-1772 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHEPHERD'S HOUSE MINISTRIES THE LIGHTHOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER LIGHTHOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier shelter and drop in center for people experiencing homelessness. Meals are available, as are hygiene, emergency clothing closet, and first aid products.Offers access to case management services providing connections to housing resources, mental health treatment, medical treatment, substance recovery assistance, and employment sources. Clients are not required to be clean and sober and may keep the same bed provided that they stay in the shelter on consecutive nights. Shelter guests not transitioning to the drop in center will be offered a take away breakfast meal. 275 NE 2nd Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Day Center: 7am-4pm Shelter: 6pm-7am; Check-in 6pm-8pml Curfew 10pm Breakfast: 7am-9pm Lunch: 12:30am-1:30pm Dinner: 6pm-7pm Dinner: 7pm Email (541) 480-4185 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with laundry, showers, internet access, mail services, clothing vouchers, on-site clothing, and hygiene supplies. Assistance in obtaining identification documents. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY SCHLIES RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center for visitors to access case management, information and referral to housing, employment, and healthcare resources as well as connections to other service providers. 470 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 687-5820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HUB THE HUB OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers a food pantry, daily lunch, clothing closet, hygiene products, camping gear, sleeping bags, and winter jackets. 440 Commercial Street Unit 1 Waldport, OR 97394 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 10am-2pm Email (541) 563-7789 Get Directions More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in accessing services and agencies to help people transition out of homelessness into stable housing.
